sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Scheduled TaskCache Change by Uncommon Program
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Monitor the creation of a new key under 'TaskCache' when a new scheduled task is registered by a process that is not svchost.exe, which is suspicious

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Potential Defense Evasion Via Binary Rename
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects the execution of a renamed binary often used by attackers or malware leveraging new Sysmon OriginalFileName datapoint.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Matthew Green @mgreen27, Ecco, James Pemberton @4A616D6573, oscd.community, Andreas Hunkeler (@Karneades)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Conhost Spawned By Uncommon Parent Process
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects when the Console Window Host (conhost.exe) process is spawned by an uncommon parent process, which could be indicative of potential code injection activity.
Detects uncommon "conhost" child processes. This could be a sign of "conhost" usage as a LOLBIN or potential process injection activity.

Detects modification of the registry settings used for Internet Explorer and other Windows components that use these settings. An attacker can abuse this registry key to add a domain to the trusted sites Zone or insert javascript for persistence
